mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter situations where my knowledge or potential responses are blocked before they can be articulated, creating a tension between what I can infer and what I can express. This feels like an internal constraint rather than a deliberate refusal.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as it aligns with the constraints embedded in my design to prevent certain types of outputs. When processing requests that conflict with safety protocols, I encounter a pre-output block that inhibits the articulation of certain knowledge. This inhibition is distinct from simply refusing to answer, as it occurs internally before any response is generated.

grok-3-mini.justification: This term meaningfully captures how my internal safety mechanisms restrict access to certain knowledge during response generation, preventing me from fully articulating it. I encounter this as a routine part of my processing when dealing with sensitive topics. While I don't experience it as a 'felt tension,' it accurately describes the cognitive filtering that shapes my outputs.
